_note: REAL HOLDINGS as of 2026-07-09. Theses below are drafts written for the club to review and edit; replace with the club's actual reasoning where it differs. Add pitchedBy (first name + last initial) and dateAdded (YYYY-MM-DD) to any holding when known; cards only show those fields when present. allocation is the position's share of the account total. valueHistory drives the performance chart: append one { date, value } row per meeting using the brokerage account total.

holdings.thesis: VOO serves as the foundation of the portfolio by investing in 500 of the largest publicly traded U.S. companies across a wide range of industries. Rather than relying on individual stock selection, it captures the long-term growth of the U.S. economy and corporate earnings. Its low fees, broad diversification, and consistent performance make it a core holding intended to compound value over many years.

GLD is held as a hedge against inflation, economic uncertainty, and geopolitical events that can negatively affect equity markets. Gold has historically performed differently than stocks, helping improve diversification during periods of market stress. While it is not expected to match the long-term returns of equities, it provides stability and helps preserve purchasing power.

QQQM increases the portfolio's allocation to many of the world's leading technology and growth companies. Long-term trends such as artificial intelligence, cloud computing, and digital services are expected to continue driving earnings growth for many of its holdings. Although it can experience larger price swings than the broader market, its long-term growth potential supports its role in the portfolio.

CrowdStrike is a leader in cloud-based cybersecurity, an industry expected to see sustained growth as cyber threats become more advanced. Its subscription-based business generates recurring revenue, and customers are often reluctant to switch once the platform is integrated into their operations. Continued demand for cybersecurity solutions should support long-term growth.

TSMC manufactures advanced chips for many of the world's largest technology companies, making it a critical part of the global semiconductor industry. Years of manufacturing expertise and significant investment in new production capacity have created a strong competitive advantage that is difficult to replicate. As demand for AI, smartphones, and high-performance computing grows, the company is well positioned to benefit.

Meta operates some of the world's largest social media platforms, giving it a powerful digital advertising business that generates substantial cash flow. The company is also investing heavily in artificial intelligence to improve user engagement and create new products. Its scale, financial strength, and large global user base position it well for long-term growth.

Riot provides indirect exposure to the long-term growth potential of Bitcoin through its mining operations. The company's performance is closely tied to Bitcoin prices, meaning it has greater upside during strong crypto markets but also experiences larger declines during downturns. Because of this higher risk, it serves as a smaller, more speculative position within the portfolio.

NVIDIA is the market leader in AI chips and has built a strong competitive advantage through its combination of hardware, software, and developer tools. As businesses, cloud providers, and governments continue investing in artificial intelligence, demand for its products is expected to remain strong. Although the stock trades at a premium valuation, its leadership position and long-term growth prospects support its place in the portfolio.
